title: Cable Reel Removal and Installation
section: Driver assistance SRS airbags seat belts

crossRefs.label: If adjustment is necessary, turn the tie rods by the same amount on both sides.

items: Proceed with the work while observing work precautions. ​
Disconnect the cable from the minus (-) terminal of the 12V battery and wait at least 3 minutes before starting work.
Ensure that the front wheel is in the straight ahead position and the steering wheel is in the straight ahead position.

blocks.text: If the airbag is deployed, the cable reel cannot be reused due to heat damage.
5. Cable Reel and rudder angle sensor removal
Release the upper and lower locks and remove the cable reel and rudder angle sensor (A).
Remove the cable reel (B) from the rudder angle sensor (A).

items: Proceed with the work while observing work precautions.
Centering of the cable reel is not necessary because the new cable reel is secured at the midpoint by a stopper.
When replacing the cable reel with a new cable reel, be sure to remove the stopper after installation.
Ensure that the front wheel is in the straight ahead position and the steering wheel is in the straight ahead position.

blocks.text: If the airbag is deployed, the cable reel cannot be reused due to heat damage.
Attach the cable reel (A) to the rudder angle sensor (B).

items: Align the projection (C) of the rudder angle sensor with the groove on the cable reel.

blocks.text: 2. Cable Reel and rudder angle sensor mounting
Push the cable reel and rudder angle sensor (A) into place until the upper and lower locks engage.

Turn the cable reel (A) clockwise (clockwise rotation) until it stops. Turn the cable reel counterclockwise (left turn) until the arrow (TOP mark) (B) points upward. Turn the cable reel 2 turns counterclockwise (left rotation) from the state of step 2, with the arrow (TOP mark) on the cable reel pointing upward, to center the cable reel.
7. Check after installation of steering wheel

items.text: Connect the cable to the (-) terminal of the 12V battery, turn the ignition to the ON mode, and check that the SRS warning light comes on for 6 seconds and then goes out. Turn the steering wheel fully to the left and right to confirm that the SRS warning light does not come on.

Connect the cable to the (-) terminal of the 12V battery, turn the ignition to the ON mode, and check that the SRS warning light comes on for 6 seconds and then goes out. Turn the steering wheel fully to the left and right to confirm that the SRS warning light does not come on.
Check the operation of the horn.
Check the operation of the horn.
Check the operation of the switches on the steering wheel.
Check the operation of the switches on the steering wheel.
